title: Iranians' daily life upended by massive strikes: 'And now, what will happen?'
subhead: Since US-Israeli airstrikes began, Iranians – cut off from the internet and information – have struggled cope amid daily explosions. The civilian death toll continues to rise.

title: Hezbollah returns to its core identity: Defending Iran
subhead: The death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has driven Hezbollah, a party and militia group founded by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, to once again enter into war against Israel.

title: France sends aircraft carrier to Mediterranean over Iran war
subhead: The United States and Israel launched attacks against Iran on Saturday, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. French President Emmanuel Macron said they 'cannot approve' of the military action 'conducted outside international law,' explaining that France forced downed drones 'in self-defense' during the opening hours of the conflict.

title: Russia powerless to help ally Iran under US-Israel strikes
subhead: After the fall of Bashar al-Assad in Syria and Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, verbal support is all Vladimir Putin can offer the Iranian government after Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death. However, Moscow could benefit from rising hydrocarbon prices.

title: At the border with Lebanon, Israelis hope for the end of Hezbollah
subhead: For the first time since 2024, the Lebanese militia launched rockets into Israeli territory overnight from Sunday to Monday. The Israeli army responded, adding a new dimension to the conflict with Iran.

Attacks on Iran: US and Israel are committing 'a crime of aggression,' says Bernie Sanders' former adviser
subhead: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'

title: The 'great replacement' theory: 'A hateful, conspiratorial phrase' now in 'everyday language'
subhead: The expression, coined by far-right ideologue Renaud Camus in 2010, is based on a myth Hitler developed. Its drift into everyday language marks a turning point, according to Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard.

title: Iranian regime scrambles to survive after Khamenei's death
subhead: Amid scenes of celebration and mourning, the formation of an interim government and ongoing bombings, Iran has entered a period of profound uncertainty, where the Islamic Republic's very survival and the reshaping of its internal balance of power are at stake.
